mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution of Kubernetes that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in enterprise environments. It leverages the Tanzu Framework to offer a more integrated experience with enhanced APIs and packaging capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is necessary for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid clusters.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the Tanzu CLI executable.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configuration.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI with default settings.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Grid cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes Grid cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Create a cluster named 'my-cluster' with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Get the kubeconfig for the admin user.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes Grid cluster
uninstall.description:
This step deletes the cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster named 'my-cluster'.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Remove the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the cluster before upgrading.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to the new Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated for the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your environment meets the minimum resource requirements (2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the cluster has not been set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node NotReady'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more nodes are not in a ready state due to resource constraints or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs and ensure that the nodes have sufficient resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified cluster does not exist or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name and ensure it has not been deleted.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI commands for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy commands in favor of new streamlined commands.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to back up your clusters and review the new CLI commands.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --num-nodes 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure your environment meets the resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
